author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods
book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon XI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It is decreed that virgins who have been betrothed, and who have afterwards been carried off by others, shall be restored to those to whom they had formerly been betrothed, even though they may have suffered violence from the ravisher.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: If a young girl who is engaged be stolen away by force by another man, let her be restored to the former.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: This canon treats only of betrothed women (of the sponsalia de futuro) not of those who are married (of the sponsalia de præsenti).

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: In the case of the latter there could be no doubt as to the duty of restitution.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: The man who was betrothed was, moreover, at liberty to receive his affianced bride who had been carried off or not.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Here Balsamon puts in a very proper cave, viz.: If he to whom she was espoused demand her to be his wife.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Compare St. Basil’s twenty-second canon in his letter to Amphilochius, where it is so ruled.
